rxn_class_summary: Organo-mediated electrochemical three-component arylcarboxylation of styrenes with aryl halides and CO2 to give 2,2-diarylpropionic-type carboxylic acids
electrochemical_role: Cathodic single-electron reduction of a naphthalene-2,6-dicarboxylate mediator (Med-1) generates a radical anion that acts as an outer-sphere SET mediator, reducing the aryl halide to an aryl radical; Mg sacrificial anode supplies Mg2+ and electrons

key_intermediates.name: Med-1 radical anion (dimethyl naphthalene-2,6-dicarboxylate radical anion)
benzyl radical from anti-Markovnikov addition to styrene

electron_flow: Cathode reduces Med-1 to its radical anion, which performs outer-sphere SET to ArX giving [ArX]•- that fragments to Ar• and X-; Ar• adds anti-Markovnikov to styrene to form a benzyl radical, which is further reduced at the cathode to a benzyl carbanion that nucleophilically attacks CO2. Mg anode is sacrificially oxidized to Mg2+.
rate_determining_step: Outer-sphere SET from Med-1 radical anion to aryl halide (ΔG‡ ≈ 21.2 kcal/mol by DFT for PhI)

probe_reagents.name: isobutyraldehyde (electrophile swap)
probe_reagents.observation: Replacing CO2 gave alcohol 60 in 21%, evidencing a benzyl carbanion intermediate

probe_reagents.name: cyclobutanone (electrophile swap)
probe_reagents.observation: Gave tertiary alcohol 61 in 12%, supporting benzyl anion intermediate

probe_reagents.name: H2O
probe_reagents.observation: 20 equiv H2O gave hydrogenation/arylation product 62 in 39% (carbanion protonation)

probe_reagents.name: D2O
probe_reagents.observation: Product 63 isolated in 37% with 92% D incorporation at benzylic position, confirming benzyl carbanion

probe_reagents.name: vinylidenecyclopropane radical clock (64)
probe_reagents.observation: Ring-opened arylcarboxylation product 65 obtained in 37% (Z/E = 1/9), supporting radical addition of Ar• to alkene

probe_reagents.name: DMPO (5,5-dimethyl-1-pyrroline N-oxide)
probe_reagents.observation: Phenyl-radical DMPO adduct observed by EPR (g=2.0058, AN=14.40 G, AH=21.18 G) and confirmed by ESI-HRMS; no EPR signal without Med-1

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry of Med-1 with PhI / styrene
probe_reagents.observation: Med-1 reduction peak at -2.47 V shows increasing catalytic current with added PhI; no change with 4-phenylstyrene, indicating Med-1 mediates SET to ArX and not to styrene

notes: DFT comparison shows SET from Med-1•- is favorable to PhI (ΔG = -5.6 kcal/mol, ΔG‡ = 21.2) but unfavorable to styrene (ΔG‡ = 27.4) and CO2 (ΔG‡ = 25.3), explaining chemoselectivity. Reactivity tuning: more electron-rich Med-7 used for aryl bromides; Med-9 (methyl benzoate) for electron-rich aryl bromides with more negative potentials. Competing pathways suppressed include hydrogenation, hydrocarboxylation, dicarboxylation, and direct reduction of styrene/ArX.
